seoTitle: How to Create a Client List in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to build a centralized client directory in ClickUp with custom fields, color-coded statuses, and filtered table views.
intro: Keeping client information scattered across spreadsheets and email threads makes it hard to get a quick picture of your accounts. This guide covers building a dedicated Client List in ClickUp, adding fields for contact details and contract tiers, and using color-coded statuses and filters to quickly browse active, onboarding, and past accounts.

steps.heading: Create a dedicated Client Directory list
steps.body: Set up a clean list separate from your project work specifically to hold client records.

steps.heading: Add custom fields for client details
steps.body: Build fields for contact emails, contract tiers, and account managers so each client profile has the essential information at a glance.

steps.heading: Set up color-coded statuses
steps.body: Use custom status colors to categorize accounts as Active, Onboarding, or Past Client.

steps.heading: Switch to Table view
steps.body: Use a high-density Table layout to organize accounts like a master spreadsheet you can scan quickly.

steps.heading: Filter by contract value or account rep
steps.body: Use the filtering ribbon to isolate specific client pools by contract value or assigned account manager.

steps.heading: Link clients to active projects
steps.body: Use relational task fields to connect your Client List to the actual project boards those accounts are tied to.

faq.question: Can I see total contract value across all my clients?
faq.answer: Yes, activating calculation footer rows at the bottom of the Table grid shows cumulative monthly contract metrics.

faq.question: How do I flag a high-priority client?
faq.answer: You can use conditional color-coding markers to instantly flag high-priority accounts or upcoming renewals.

faq.question: Can I connect a client record to their active project?
faq.answer: Yes, relational task fields let you link a client's directory entry to the actual project board or task list associated with their account.

faq.question: Can I export my client list to share with the team?
faq.answer: Yes, you can export a custom-filtered client sheet to share with administrative team partners while keeping the master database secure.
